data.title: Binance Alpha Launches PROMPT
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to the official interface, Binance Alpha has launched PROMPT.

data.title: U.S. March Consumer Prices Decline Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: According to Odaily, consumer prices in the United States unexpectedly fell in March, despite U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff reductions on other countries, which tend to increase inflation risks. The U.S. Department of Labor's Bureau of Labor Statistics reported on Thursday that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) decreased by 0.1% in March, following a 0.2% rise in February. This decline may reflect a drop in energy costs and the fading impact of price increases earlier in the year. Excluding the volatile food and energy sectors, the core CPI rose by 0.1% in March, compared to a 0.2% increase in February. Year-over-year, the core CPI increased by 2.8% in March, down from a 3.1% rise in February. The March data likely reflects only a small portion of the initial wave of Trump's import tariffs. Capital Economics estimates that the inflation rate will peak at around 4%, double the Federal Reserve's 2% target. Minutes from the Federal Reserve's March 18-19 meeting, released on Wednesday, indicate that policymakers almost unanimously agree that the economy faces the dual risks of rising inflation and slowing growth.

data.title: OKX will officially launch PROMPT/USDT perpetual contract on the web, App and API
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, OKX will officially launch the PROMPT/USDT perpetual contract on the web, App and API at 9:30 pm (UTC+8) on April 10, 2025, and will launch PROMPT's leveraged trading and simple coin earning at 12:10 noon on April 11, 2025 (UTC+8).

data.title: The unexpected decline in U.S. CPI in March reflects the gradual fading of the impact of price increases at the beginning of the year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News U.S. consumer prices unexpectedly fell in March, even as Trump lowered tariffs on other countries, inflation risks tended to rise. The U.S. Department of Labor's Bureau of Labor Statistics announced on Thursday that the CPI fell 0.1% month-on-month in March, compared with a 0.2% increase in February. The decline may reflect the gradual fading impact of lower energy costs and price increases at the beginning of the year. Excluding the volatile food and energy sectors, the core CPI rose 0.1% month-on-month in March and 0.2% in February. From a year-on-year perspective, the core CPI rose 2.8% in March and 3.1% in February. The March data may only reflect a small part of Trump's first wave of import tariffs. Capital Economics estimates that inflation will peak at around 4%, twice the Fed's 2% target. The minutes of the Fed's March 18-19 meeting released on Wednesday showed that policymakers were almost unanimous that the economy faces the risk of rising inflation and slowing growth. (Jinshi)

data.title: After the release of US CPI, the market almost fully priced in the Fed's June rate cut
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: After the release of the US CPI, the market almost fully priced in the Fed's June rate cut. (Jinshi)

data.title: DarkNode, a privacy-first encryption application platform built on BSC, is about to be officially launched
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: DarkNode, a privacy-first encryption application platform built on BSC, announced that it will be officially launched soon. DarkNode is committed to achieving transaction privacy protection and uses the enhanced Mimblewimble protocol to achieve transaction privacy.
In this protocol system, all transaction input and output content are integrated into a single net difference transaction, which makes the transaction map completely hidden.
In addition, DarkNode supports one-time stealth addresses, which can effectively prevent addresses from being reused, thereby preventing transaction data from being tracked across chains. At the same time, DarkNode is organically combined with Dandelion++ network anonymity technology to mask IP addresses in the path of transaction propagation, fully ensuring on-chain privacy security from the transaction layer to the network layer, and achieving full-chain privacy protection.

data.title: Bitget Wallet launches token contract security detection function, supporting six major chains
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Web3 wallet Bitget Wallet announced the official launch of the token contract security detection function to further enhance the security of user transactions. Currently, it supports token contract risk identification on mainstream chains such as Ethereum, Solana, BNB Chain, Base, Polygon, and Arbitrum. Users can click the "Security Detection" function on the token K-line page to view key parameters such as contract permissions, position distribution, and token destruction ratio, and fully understand the possible risks of tokens before trading.
The Bitget Wallet contract detection function aims to enhance users' transparency and judgment of trading tokens and reduce asset risks caused by information asymmetry. The system supports automatic identification of common potential risk factors on multiple chains. In the future, it will further expand support for more public chains and continue to optimize the detection model to provide users with a safer trading environment.

data.title: U.S. stock index futures narrow losses
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The U.S. CPI rose 2.4% year-on-year in March, while economists had expected a 2.6% increase. The core CPI rose 2.8% year-on-year, lower than the expected 3%. The lower-than-expected inflation data reinforced the market's expectations that the Federal Reserve will continue to cut interest rates this year, and U.S. stock index futures narrowed their early losses. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Weekly Jobless Claims Reach 223,000 in Early April
data.description: According to Odaily, the number of initial jobless claims in the United States for the week ending April 5 reached 223,000. This figure aligns with expectations and shows an increase from the previous week's count of 219,000.

data.title: U.S. Short-Term Interest Rate Futures Rise Following CPI Data Release
data.description: According to PANews, following the release of the U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) data, short-term interest rate futures in the United States experienced an increase. This development has led traders to intensify their bets on a potential interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve.
